title: Introduction
description: NOC 63101 – Real estate agents and salespersons | GTR Immigration Real estate agents and salespersons under NOC 63101 are the facilitators of Canada’s dynamic property market. From helping families find their first homes to negotiating major commercial leases, these TEER 2 professionals are vital to the national economy. With Canada’s high focus on immigration and housing development, real estate expertise is in high demand. GTR Immigration helps global real estate professionals leverage their sales track record to qualify for Permanent Residency.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level2Major Group63 – Occupations in personal serviceSub-Major Group631 – Insurance, real estate and financial sales occupationsMinor Group6310 – Insurance, real estate and financial sales occupationsNOC Code63101

duties: Solicit property sales listings from prospective vendors.
Assist vendors in selling property by establishing a target price and advertising the property.
Assist prospective buyers in selecting, visiting, inspecting, and making offers on properties.
Advise clients on market conditions, prices, mortgages, and legal requirements.
Draw up sales agreements and coordinate with legal and financial institutions.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of a real estate training course is mandatory.
Provincial or territorial licensure is required to practice.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate to Favourable. While market fluctuations occur, the long-term trend of population growth and urbanization in Canada ensures a constant need for real estate services.
labour_market_demand: Canada’s real estate market remains one of the most active in the world. Demand is particularly high in Ontario, British Columbia, and Alberta. As the government increases housing targets, the need for ethical and skilled agents to navigate new developments and resale markets is critical.
Thriving Market: Access to one of the most stable and high-value real estate sectors globally.
Self-Employment Freedom: High rates of independence and the ability to build your own brand.
Networking: Collaborate with developers, lawyers, and mortgage specialists in a professional ecosystem.
pathways: Federal Skilled Worker (Express Entry): A popular choice for high-performing sales professionals.
Self-Employed Persons Program: For world-class agents with significant independent experience.
Provincial Nominee Program (PNP): Selective opportunities in provinces with high growth targets.
